# gics_classifications.feather

## Description
Complete mapping of GICS (Global Industry Classification Standard) codes to their descriptive titles. Includes the full hierarchy: Sector (2-digit), Group (4-digit), Industry (6-digit), and Sub-Industry (8-digit) levels.

This dataset provides descriptive titles for all GICS classification codes used in the Compustat database. It can be used to map GICS codes in the main dataset (gics_sector, gics_group, gics_industry, gics_subindustry) to their human-readable names.

## Generated by
`src/pre_query/compustat/download_industry_classifications.py`

## Variables

### giccd
GICS classification code. String type, zero-padded to 8 digits. The code length indicates the hierarchy level:
- 2 digits: Sector (e.g., "10" = Energy)
- 4 digits: Group (e.g., "1010" = Energy Equipment & Services)
- 6 digits: Industry (e.g., "101010" = Oil & Gas Drilling)
- 8 digits: Sub-Industry (e.g., "10101010" = Oil & Gas Drilling)

### gicdesc
GICS classification description/title. String type. The human-readable name for the classification code (e.g., "Energy", "Oil & Gas Drilling").

### gictype
GICS classification type. Category type. Indicates the hierarchy level: "Sector", "Group", "Industry", or "Sub-Industry".

### level
Classification level (code length). Integer type. Calculated from the length of giccd:
- 2: Sector
- 4: Group
- 6: Industry
- 8: Sub-Industry
